title: Baby batch 1: boilerplate sweep (5 WINs) + WIN-006 verdict boundary note + WIN-033 southern stars + precession additions
target: data/wins.json — 5 WINs (019, 021, 029, 047, 055) stale monitoring boilerplate replacement; WIN-006 verdict boundary clarification; WIN-033 detail_evidence southern-star and precession one-liners

patches.old_string: the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable.
patches.new_string: there is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-019 appears only as a static entry in the dome WIN registry — so even the nominal test that would check the hardcoded value against a live fetch is absent.
patches.description: ISS-1841 cluster A (monitoring=hardcoded): replace stale boilerplate with WIN-016/017 template. WIN-019 (Solar Analemma Figure-8 Loop Ratio) is a static entry in raw-text/02-wins.txt:101.

patches.old_string: The legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b requires that the data be "not plausibly known to the author at write-time," which is not the case for indexed public datasets.
patches.new_string: The legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b requires that the data be "not plausibly known to the author at write-time," which is not the case for indexed public datasets. Verdict boundary note: WIN-006 uses “Std Model Explains” rather than “Misleading” (WIN-007, WIN-022) because the pre-1990 linear drift is accurately described at face value — both models fit the same linear trend, so there is no false causal claim, only an absence of discriminating power. WIN-007 and WIN-022 are “Misleading” because they introduce specific causal framings (“aetheric threshold crossing,” “phase transition”) for the post-1990 acceleration that are category errors: the dome asserts a mechanistic explanation it cannot derive, not merely a shared observation.
patches.description: ISS-1913: add brief verdict-boundary note to WIN-006 detail_verdict_text explaining why WIN-006 is Std-Model-Explains while WINs 007/022 are Misleading.

patches.old_string: The magnitude difference is entirely intrinsic luminosity.
patches.new_string: The magnitude difference is entirely intrinsic luminosity. Three bright southern stars — Canopus (α Car, V=−0.74, F0Ib supergiant, ~13,600 L☉, 310 ly), Achernar (α Eri, V=0.46, B6Vep, ~3,150 L☉, 139 ly), and Acrux (α Cru, V=0.77, B0.5IV+B1V, ~25,000 L☉, 321 ly) — are all intrinsically far more luminous than Sigma Octantis, immediately falsifying any dome claim that the south polar region systematically suppresses stellar brightness: these stars are brighter, not dimmer, than dome geometry would predict if radial aetheric depth caused extinction. Earth’s axial precession (~25,772-year cycle, driven by luni-solar torques) is moving the south celestial pole toward Canopus; in approximately 6,000 years Canopus will serve as a far brighter south pole star than Sigma Octantis is today — demonstrating that the apparent dimness near the south pole is a transient coincidence of current-epoch stellar geometry, not a structural feature required by dome geometry.
patches.description: ISS-1914+ISS-1915 combined: append two sentences to WIN-033 detail_evidence. First (ISS-1914): three bright southern stars falsify aetheric dimming from first principles. Second (ISS-1915): precession sentence closes the dangling (see precession point below) reference in detail_verdict_text.

obe_resolution_details.ISS-1860: PO.DAAC domain (podaac.jpl.nasa.gov) has timed out on multiple consecutive sandbox runs. Persistent IP-side rate-limit, not a PO.DAAC site failure. No wins.json/sections.json content depends on a live PO.DAAC fetch. OBE: sandbox rate-limit.
obe_resolution_details.ISS-1922: Decider already decided audit-trail-only at ISS creation. The four sub_rationale fields (WIN-047/048/052/054) already contain [audit-trail only — not rendered] annotation. The See Part 6.13 cross-reference within the unrendered field is harmless. OBE: decider audit-trail-only decision already documented.
obe_resolution_details.ISS-1925: WIN-066 detail_evidence already reads: The +0.34 W/m2 figure cited by the dome is a decadal trend in Earth energy imbalance (Loeb et al. 2025 PNAS). The dome DOES explicitly cite this value. Counter language in detail_verdict_text is accurate. OBE: already correctly handled.

tldr_evidence: Five WINs had stale post-hoc boilerplate replaced in WIN-016 by ISS-1839 but not propagated. WIN-006 needed a verdict-boundary note explaining Std-Model-Explains vs Misleading for the NMP cluster. WIN-033 detail_evidence was missing two c2-flagged arguments: bright-southern-stars falsification and the precession epoch-coincidence argument. Three OBE dismissals: PO.DAAC sandbox rate-limit, sub_rationale audit-trail-only already documented, WIN-066 +0.34 citation already present.
tldr_verdict: Seven verification items in one batch: 5 boilerplate text replacements + 2 additive detail_evidence insertions. Three OBE dismissals. Verdict classes unchanged for all affected WINs.
notes: PATCH-1914-1915-WIN033-combined combines ISS-1914 and ISS-1915 into a single patch appending two sentences after the existing detail_evidence endpoint. Decider should apply the patches in patch_id order. PATCH-1913 appends to WIN-006 detail_verdict_text and should not affect existing test assertions.
